2605.09129.title: Data-driven Circuit Discovery for Interpretability of Language Models
2605.09129.paper_summary: Circuit discovery aims to explain how language models (LMs) implement a specific task by localizing and interpreting a circuit, a computational subgraph responsible for the LM's behavior. Existing circuit discovery methods are hypothesis-driven; they first informally define a task with a dataset, and then apply a circuit discovery algorithm over that dataset to obtain a single circuit. This imposes two strong assumptions: that the LM implements the task with a single circuit, and that the dataset adequately represents the task as humans understand it. We systematically test these assumptions across four previously studied tasks and find that even minor dataset variations that preserve task semantics can produce circuits with low edge overlap and cross-dataset faithfulness. More strikingly, when applied to a mixed dataset with two distinct tasks whose separately discovered circuits have near-zero cross-faithfulness, existing methods still return a single circuit with high faithfulness across both tasks. This indicates that current methods discover dataset-specific circuits, rather than general task circuits. We propose Data-driven Circuit Discovery (DCD), a new discovery framework that drops both assumptions: instead of returning a single circuit for a dataset, DCD first clusters examples in the dataset by how similarly the model processes them and discovers a separate circuit for each group. This allows distinct mechanisms to appear separately rather than merged into a single circuit; each circuit explains its group, not the full task. Experiments show that DCD discovers multiple circuits per dataset, each more faithful to its group than a single circuit discovered by existing methods. Broadly, DCD lets the data reveal mechanistic structure within LMs, rather than relying on human-defined task boundaries that may not align with how models organize their computation.
2605.09129.paper_summary_zh: 電路發現旨在解釋語言模型（LMs）如何通過定位和解釋一個電路來實現特定任務，這個電路是負責LM行為的計算子圖。現有的電路發現方法是以假設為驅動；它們首先用數據集非正式地定義一個任務，然後在該數據集上應用電路發現算法以獲得一個單一的電路。這施加了兩個強假設：即LM用單一電路實現任務，並且該數據集充分代表了人類理解的任務。我們系統地測試這些假設，涵蓋四個先前研究的任務，發現即使是保留任務語義的輕微數據集變化也可以產生邊緣重疊低和跨數據集忠實度低的電路。更引人注目的是，當應用於一個混合數據集，該數據集包含兩個不同任務，其分別發現的電路幾乎沒有跨忠實度時，現有方法仍然返回一個在兩個任務上都具有高忠實度的單一電路。這表明當前的方法發現的是特定於數據集的電路，而不是通用的任務電路。我們提出數據驅動電路發現（DCD），這是一個新的發現框架，放棄了這兩個假設：DCD不再為數據集返回單一電路，而是首先根據模型處理示例的相似程度對數據集中的示例進行聚類，並為每個組發現一個單獨的電路。這使得不同的機制可以分開出現，而不是合併成一個單一的電路；每個電路解釋其組，而不是整個任務。實驗顯示，DCD為每個數據集發現多個電路，每個電路對其組的忠實度都高於現有方法發現的單一電路。總體而言，DCD讓數據揭示LM內部的機械結構，而不是依賴於可能與模型組織其計算方式不一致的人類定義的任務邊界。
2605.09129.author: Daking Rai et.al.
2605.09129.authors: Daking Rai, Mor Geva, Ziyu Yao
